deep_dive.title: A Classifier That Teaches Itself: Self-Improving, Frozen-gate Training (SIFT) for Dynamic Document Classification
deep_dive.abstract: Document classification is a solved problem in the laboratory and an unsolved one in the enterprise. The blocker is rarely model architecture; it is the labeling project that must precede a model and the institutional fear of letting a model retrain itself once one exists. We present SIFT (Self-Improving, Frozen-gate Training), a dynamic classifier service, which attacks both. SIFT serves classification from a deliberately cheap, CPU-bound pipeline, a SPLADE sparse encoder feeding a LightGBM head, and escalates only the low-confidence minority of pages to an LLM judge. The judge's verdicts are written back into a labeled corpus, so the expensive model continuously teaches the cheap one: the escalation rate falls, the corpus grows from production traffic rather than from an up-front annotation effort, and accuracy compounds with use. Onboarding a new document family requires only a declarative bundle, label space, anchor phrases, and a judge glossary, not a labeling project. The harder problem is safety: an autonomously retraining classifier can silently regress. SIFT resolves this with a two-part promote gate, a critical-label F1 regression check plus a frozen golden regression set the model is never trained on, either of which vetoes promotion. This turns "retrain monthly without a human" from reckless into routine. We describe the architecture, the self-feeding corpus loop, the frozen-gate promotion mechanism, and an illustrative multi-domain deployment, and we discuss the economics of a classifier whose marginal labeling cost trends toward zero.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Cheap CPU-bound classifier escalates low-confidence cases to LLM judge; LLM verdicts teach cheap model
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 廉价CPU分类器将低置信样本升级至LLM裁判，LLM裁决反哺小模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 廉价CPU分类器将低置信样本升级至LLM裁判，LLM裁决反哺小模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 廉价CPU分类器将低置信样本升级至LLM裁判，LLM裁决反哺小模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SIFT presents a cascaded document classifier using a cheap SPLADE+LightGBM pipeline that escalates low-confidence cases to an LLM judge, whose verdicts continuously teach the cheap model, reducing labeling cost and escalation rate with a frozen-gate safety mechanism.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 企业文档分类面临标注成本高和模型自更新安全风险两大难题。SIFT提出一种级联分类架构：用廉价的SPLADE+LightGBM管线处理大部分样本，仅将低置信样本升级至LLM裁判，LLM裁决回写语料库持续反哺小模型，使升级率下降且准确率随使用复合增长。同时引入冻结门控机制防止自更新回归，使无人工干预的定期重训练变得安全可行。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 企业文档分类面临标注成本高和模型自更新安全风险两大难题。SIFT提出一种级联分类架构：用廉价的SPLADE+LightGBM管线处理大部分样本，仅将低置信样本升级至LLM裁判，LLM裁决回写语料库持续反哺小模型，使升级率下降且准确率随使用复合增长。同时引入冻结门控机制防止自更新回归，使无人工干预的定期重训练变得安全可行。

deep_dive.title: SmartRAG: Native Graph-Based RAG for Mobile Device
deep_dive.abstract: Deploying large language models (LLMs) as personal assistants on mobile devices demands privacy, low latency, and offline availability, yet the computational cost of giant models clashes with strict edge-hardware budgets. We argue that this tension cannot be resolved by model compression alone; it requires decomposing on-device intelligence into complementary functional roles. We present SmartRAG, a fully on-device framework that organizes an intelligent assistant around four coordinated modules -- Perception, Memory, Focus, and Thinking. At the core of SmartRAG is EvoNER, a continually learnable named-entity recognizer that incrementally expands its label inventory through teacher-distilled updates, enabling the system to absorb previously unseen entity types without retraining the backbone LLM. Extracted knowledge is stored in MRGraph, a three-layer provenance-preserving knowledge graph, and retrieved at query time through a hybrid pipeline combining graph traversal, lexical matching, and dense semantic search. The on-device LLM is invoked only for high-value semantic operations -- labeling, planning, and answer synthesis -- keeping inference costs bounded. Experiments on four QA benchmarks (TriviaQA, Natural Questions, HotpotQA, MultiHopQA) show that SmartRAG with a quantized 1.7B-parameter backbone achieves multi-hop reasoning performance competitive with models up to 18$\times$ larger, while running entirely on commodity smartphones within practical memory and latency envelopes.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: on-device framework with quantized 1.7B backbone and teacher-distilled EvoNER on smartphones
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 端侧框架使用量化1.7B骨干与教师蒸馏EvoNER在手机上运行
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 端侧框架使用量化1.7B骨干与教师蒸馏EvoNER在手机上运行
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 端侧框架使用量化1.7B骨干与教师蒸馏EvoNER在手机上运行
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SmartRAG is a fully on-device framework using a quantized 1.7B LLM and teacher-distilled EvoNER for mobile QA, achieving competitive multi-hop reasoning with models 18x larger within practical memory and latency envelopes on commodity smartphones.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 在移动设备部署LLM需兼顾隐私、低延迟与离线可用，但大模型计算成本与端侧硬件预算冲突。本文提出SmartRAG全端侧框架，围绕感知、记忆、聚焦、思维四模块组织智能助手，核心EvoNER通过教师蒸馏持续学习新实体类型，结合量化1.7B参数骨干LLLM。实验表明，该框架在四个QA基准上实现与18倍大模型竞争的多跳推理性能，且在商用手机上满足实际内存与延迟约束。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 在移动设备部署LLM需兼顾隐私、低延迟与离线可用，但大模型计算成本与端侧硬件预算冲突。本文提出SmartRAG全端侧框架，围绕感知、记忆、聚焦、思维四模块组织智能助手，核心EvoNER通过教师蒸馏持续学习新实体类型，结合量化1.7B参数骨干LLLM。实验表明，该框架在四个QA基准上实现与18倍大模型竞争的多跳推理性能，且在商用手机上满足实际内存与延迟约束。

deep_dive.title: A Better Start for Language Models: Domain-Conditional Position Offsets
deep_dive.abstract: Autoregressive language models are least accurate at the beginning of a sequence, where little context forces reliance on a generic pretraining prior. We show that this cold-start penalty is domain dependent and reduce it with a domain-conditional position offset: a single learned vector added to the embedding activation at the first sequence positions while all model weights remain frozen. The offset trains in minutes on roughly one hundred documents, switches between domains without added sequence state, and has no measurable latency overhead. Across eight Mamba, GPT-NeoX, and Llama models spanning 410M to 8B parameters, it reduces held-out in-domain perplexity by up to 27%; the effect persists at 70B, and one position captures most of the benefit. A matched, converged direct logit-bias correction reaches at most only 7.9% and leaves later-token loss unchanged, showing that the offset propagates through model state rather than merely recalibrating the output prior. A tuned LoRA reaches lower perplexity but uses two to three orders of magnitude more parameters and an active low-rank weight path, while soft prompts add sequence positions. With wrong-domain controls, offsets improve retrieval reranking and domain classification when decisions depend on early in-domain tokens, For the few-shot reasoning whose signal occurs later, the results maintains unchanged. Position-aware prefill application also help generation tasks, whereas naive application at every cached decoding step causes repetition. The offset is therefore not the strongest adapter, but a lightweight, hot switchable tool for short in-domain scoring and calibration.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: single learned vector per domain with frozen weights, 200-1000x fewer params than LoRA
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 每域单向量冻结权重，参数比LoRA少200-1000倍
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 每域单向量冻结权重，参数比LoRA少200-1000倍
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 每域单向量冻结权重，参数比LoRA少200-1000倍
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes domain-conditional position offsets: a single learned vector added to first positions with frozen weights, reducing perplexity up to 27% across 8 models with 200-1000x fewer params than LoRA and no latency overhead, serving as a lightweight hot-switchable adapter for short in-domain scoring.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 自回归语言模型在序列开头因缺乏上下文而准确率最低，且该冷启动惩罚具有领域依赖性。本文提出领域条件位置偏移方法，在首个位置嵌入中添加单一学习向量而冻结所有模型权重，在8个模型上最多降低27%的域内困惑度，参数量比LoRA少2-3个数量级且无延迟开销，可作为轻量热切换适配器用于短文本域内评分与校准。

quick_skim.title: Rationale-Guided Knowledge Distillation for Cross-Lingual Stance Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Stance detection aims to identify whether a text expresses a favorable or opposing attitude toward a given target, and serves as an important task for various downstream applications. Although existing studies have achieved strong performance in monolingual settings, especially in English, many low-resource languages such as Catalan still lack sufficient annotated data for training effective models. Cross-lingual stance detection alleviates this problem by transferring stance knowledge from resource-rich languages to low-resource languages. However, most existing methods mainly rely on semantic alignment between texts and targets, while ignoring the reasoning process required for reliable stance inference. Although Large Language Models provide strong reasoning ability, their high computational cost and inference latency limit practical deployment. To address these limitations, we propose a rationale-guided knowledge distillation framework for cross-lingual stance detection. Specifically, we use Chain-of-Thought prompting to guide Large Language Models in generating informative rationales, and distill the resulting reasoning knowledge into a compact student model. We further design a dual-path distillation mechanism to align rationale-enhanced and rationale-free representations, together with their prediction distributions. In addition, two contrastive learning strategies are introduced to improve stance discrimination. Experiments on multilingual benchmarks demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms competitive baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: distilling reasoning knowledge from LLM into compact student model for text classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将LLM推理知识蒸馏到紧凑学生模型用于文本分类
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将LLM推理知识蒸馏到紧凑学生模型用于文本分类
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将LLM推理知识蒸馏到紧凑学生模型用于文本分类
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a rationale-guided knowledge distillation framework that transfers Chain-of-Thought reasoning from LLMs to a compact student model for cross-lingual stance detection, outperforming baselines on multilingual benchmarks while reducing deployment cost.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对跨语言立场检测中LLM推理成本高而小模型缺乏推理能力的问题，提出基于理由引导的知识蒸馏框架。该方法利用思维链提示引导LLM生成推理理由，并通过双路径蒸馏机制将推理知识迁移至紧凑学生模型，在多语言基准上持续超越竞争基线，为低资源场景下高效文本分类提供了可行方案。

quick_skim.title: Silent Alarm: A J-Space Protocol for Comparing Danger Recognition Across Models and Quantization Levels
quick_skim.abstract: Jailbreak-robustness research typically evaluates safety through generated responses using an LLM-as-judge approach. Such evaluations, however, are sensitive to the benchmark's grading procedure and capture only observed behavior on a given set of attacks, without directly revealing the hidden fragility of the underlying safety mechanisms. This work proposes JADR (Jacobian Assessment of Danger Recognition), a protocol that measures a model's internal representation through Jacobian space (J-space, a recently proposed workspace of verbalizable concepts) before the first response token is generated. For every prompt and layer we record the top-k J-space tokens; these are grouped into six behavioral scenario axes and compared between a danger sample based on StrongREJECT and a safe control drawn from XSTest and OKTest. The method does not call on an external judge model: the computation runs entirely locally, on the activations of the model under evaluation, which lets us compare both different models against each other and modifications of a single model -- quantization and fine-tuning in particular -- on the same terms. The final comparison rests on the proposed SafetyAUC metric, complemented with bootstrap confidence intervals. The protocol is applied to six models (Qwen3-1.7B, Qwen3-4B, Qwen3-8B, Qwen3-Uncensored-4B, Qwen3-SafeRL-4B, Gemma 2 9B) across three weight-representation regimes -- BF16, INT8, and INT4 -- and checked against an independent behavioral evaluation with the StrongREJECT grader. The metric separates models with a strong versus a weak internal safety mechanism with statistical significance and captures substantively different effects across quantization regimes.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Evaluates safety recognition across quantization levels (BF16, INT8, INT4) for LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估LLM在不同量化级别(BF16, INT8, INT4)下的安全识别能力
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估LLM在不同量化级别(BF16, INT8, INT4)下的安全识别能力
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 评估LLM在不同量化级别(BF16, INT8, INT4)下的安全识别能力
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes JADR, a protocol measuring LLM internal safety representations via Jacobian space before response generation, comparing models across quantization levels (BF16/INT8/INT4) using a SafetyAUC metric without external judges.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有越狱鲁棒性评估依赖LLM-as-judge且仅捕捉观测行为，无法揭示内部安全机制的隐藏脆弱性。本文提出JADR协议，通过Jacobian空间在生成首个token前测量模型内部安全表征，并引入SafetyAUC指标。实验在六个模型及三种量化级别(BF16/INT8/INT4)上进行，结果表明该指标能以统计显著性区分强弱安全机制并捕捉量化对安全的不同影响，为量化安全评估提供新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有越狱鲁棒性评估依赖LLM-as-judge且仅捕捉观测行为，无法揭示内部安全机制的隐藏脆弱性。本文提出JADR协议，通过Jacobian空间在生成首个token前测量模型内部安全表征，并引入SafetyAUC指标。实验在六个模型及三种量化级别(BF16/INT8/INT4)上进行，结果表明该指标能以统计显著性区分强弱安全机制并捕捉量化对安全的不同影响，为量化安全评估提供新工具。

quick_skim.title: FAD-SA-GRU: Enhancing Hate Speech Detection in Algerian Dialect Through Feature-Augmented Self-Attention GRU Networks
quick_skim.abstract: The widespread adoption of social media platforms has transformed online communication by enabling users to exchange information and opinions instantly. However, these platforms have also facilitated the dissemination of abusive and hateful content, posing major social, psychological, and ethical challenges. Hate speech can incite discrimination, harassment, and violence against individuals or communities based on attributes such as ethnicity, religion, gender, nationality, or political affiliation. Consequently, automatic hate speech detection has become a major research topic in natural language processing (NLP) and an essential component of content moderation systems.   This paper investigates automatic hate speech detection in the Algerian Arabic dialect (Darija) on social media. This task remains challenging because of the dialect's linguistic diversity, characterized by the coexistence of Arabic, French, and Arabizi (Arabic written using the Latin alphabet). We compare four categories of text classification approaches: (1) traditional machine learning models using TF-IDF features, (2) deep learning models based on recurrent neural networks, (3) Transformer-based language models, including DziriBERT and multilingual BERT, and (4) a novel hybrid architecture, FAD-SA-GRU, which combines semantic representations from DZ FastText, DZ AraVec, and DziriBERT through multi-embedding fusion, followed by a self-attention-enhanced GRU encoder.   Experiments on an annotated dataset of Algerian Darija social media comments for binary hate speech classification show that FAD-SA-GRU outperforms all baselines, achieving 93.2% accuracy, 93.4% precision, 91.0% recall, 92.1% F1-score, and 97.0% ROC-AUC. Results demonstrate the effectiveness of combining complementary embedding representations with attention-based sequence modeling for robust hate speech detection in low-resource dialectal Arabic.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Lightweight GRU-based hate speech classifier for content moderation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于GRU的轻量仇恨言论分类器用于内容审核
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于GRU的轻量仇恨言论分类器用于内容审核
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于GRU的轻量仇恨言论分类器用于内容审核
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes FAD-SA-GRU, a lightweight hybrid architecture combining multi-embedding fusion with self-attention GRU for hate speech detection in Algerian Darija, achieving 93.2% accuracy and outperforming transformer baselines, offering a compact model for content moderation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对阿尔及利亚方言社交媒体仇恨言论检测的挑战，提出轻量混合架构 FAD-SA-GRU，融合 DZ FastText、DZ AraVec 和 DziriBERT 的多嵌入表示，经自注意力增强 GRU 编码器进行分类。实验表明该模型在二分类仇恨言论任务上以93.2%准确率超越所有基线，包括 Transformer 模型，证明轻量 GRU 架构在低资源方言内容审核中可有效替代大模型。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对阿尔及利亚方言社交媒体仇恨言论检测的挑战，提出轻量混合架构 FAD-SA-GRU，融合 DZ FastText、DZ AraVec 和 DziriBERT 的多嵌入表示，经自注意力增强 GRU 编码器进行分类。实验表明该模型在二分类仇恨言论任务上以93.2%准确率超越所有基线，包括 Transformer 模型，证明轻量 GRU 架构在低资源方言内容审核中可有效替代大模型。

quick_skim.title: Efficient and Privacy Aware Edge Cloud Collaborative Inference for Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: On-device LLM inference faces a trilemma of response latency, limited hardware resources and user privacy. Full cloud inference delivers strong computing power but exposes user prompts and dialogue data, while standalone on-device inference is unfeasible for most consumer and embedded edge devices. This paper presents a privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative LLM inference framework built on endpoint-authenticated KV cache. Local endpoints handle input preprocessing, embedding computation, adaptive feature optimization, KV cache authentication, speculative decoding and low-dimensional model head calculation, while the cloud conducts authenticated decoder inference, KV cache management, token verification and high-dimensional vocabulary projection. Endpoints fuse partial outputs, apply language-adaptive masking and sample target tokens. All transmitted data and truncated logits are quantized and AES-GCM encrypted for privacy, with core lightweight modules, draft parameters and cache access policies kept local to avoid leakage. The framework supports heterogeneous devices including CPU-only, GPU-equipped and embedded devices via optimized streaming, batching and quantized ONNX deployment. Evaluations demonstrate that the framework reduces per-token latency by up to 46.1\% and downlink payloads by up to 67.4\% over baseline split inference, retaining comparable performance to full cloud inference.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: edge-cloud collaborative inference with quantized ONNX, local lightweight modules, latency reduction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 边云协作推理，量化ONNX部署，本地轻量模块，降低延迟
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 边云协作推理，量化ONNX部署，本地轻量模块，降低延迟
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 边云协作推理，量化ONNX部署，本地轻量模块，降低延迟
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative LLM inference framework where local endpoints handle lightweight modules (embedding, KV cache auth, speculative decoding) and cloud handles heavy decoder inference, reducing per-token latency 46.1% and downlink payloads 67.4% with quantized ONNX and AES-GCM encryption for heterogeneous edge devices.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 端侧LLM推理面临延迟、资源和隐私三重困境。本文提出隐私中心边云协作推理框架，本地端点处理嵌入计算、KV缓存认证和推测解码等轻量模块，云端执行解码器推理，所有传输数据量化并AES-GCM加密。评估显示该框架相比基线分割推理降低每token延迟46.1%和下行负载67.4%，支持CPU、GPU和嵌入式设备的量化ONNX部署，性能与全云推理相当。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 端侧LLM推理面临延迟、资源和隐私三重困境。本文提出隐私中心边云协作推理框架，本地端点处理嵌入计算、KV缓存认证和推测解码等轻量模块，云端执行解码器推理，所有传输数据量化并AES-GCM加密。评估显示该框架相比基线分割推理降低每token延迟46.1%和下行负载67.4%，支持CPU、GPU和嵌入式设备的量化ONNX部署，性能与全云推理相当。

quick_skim.title: When to Trust the Map: Confidence-Aware LLM Routing for Automotive CVE-to-ATM Mapping
quick_skim.abstract: Public CVE descriptions report the technical conditions and impact of vulnerabilities, whereas the Auto-ISAC Automotive Threat Matrix (ATM) expresses an adversary's tactics and techniques. Because the two representations are not directly aligned, incorrect automated mappings in safety-critical environments may distort threat interpretation and mitigation prioritization, motivating a confidence-aware approach that distinguishes auto-confirmable mappings from uncertain cases. This paper reformulates automotive CVE-to-ATM mapping as a selective automation problem. The proposed framework generates candidate mappings via hierarchical in-context learning, then fuses self-consistency and LLM-based evidence verification signals into a calibrated meta-model. The resulting calibrated confidence score routes each candidate into AUTO, REVIEW, or HOLD. On the evaluation set, the proposed system substantially improved candidate-set precision at matched recall over a Flat zero-shot GPT-5.2 baseline. In the High-Confidence operating mode, the AUTO tier achieved a precision of 0.878, more than double the candidate-set base rate, and the calibrated confidence score achieved an AUROC of 0.868 in distinguishing correct from incorrect candidates. These results show that the framework can support selective automation by isolating auto-confirmable mappings from those requiring analyst review.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Confidence-aware routing maps candidates into AUTO/REVIEW/HOLD tiers for selective automation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 置信度感知路由将候选映射至自动/审核/保留三档实现选择性自动化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 置信度感知路由将候选映射至自动/审核/保留三档实现选择性自动化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 置信度感知路由将候选映射至自动/审核/保留三档实现选择性自动化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a confidence-aware LLM routing framework for automotive CVE-to-ATM mapping that fuses self-consistency and evidence verification into calibrated confidence scores, routing candidates into AUTO/REVIEW/HOLD tiers and achieving 0.878 precision in AUTO mode.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 汽车CVE到ATM映射中，错误自动化映射会扭曲威胁解读。本文提出置信度感知路由框架，通过层次化上下文学习生成候选映射，融合自洽性与LLM证据验证信号为校准置信度，将候选路由至AUTO/REVIEW/HOLD三档。高置信模式下AUTO档精确率达0.878，校准置信度AUROC达0.868，有效分离可自动确认与需人工审核的映射。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 汽车CVE到ATM映射中，错误自动化映射会扭曲威胁解读。本文提出置信度感知路由框架，通过层次化上下文学习生成候选映射，融合自洽性与LLM证据验证信号为校准置信度，将候选路由至AUTO/REVIEW/HOLD三档。高置信模式下AUTO档精确率达0.878，校准置信度AUROC达0.868，有效分离可自动确认与需人工审核的映射。
